competitor_patterns: Top-ranking content (road.cc, Optica Bassol) is significantly longer — 10,000–39,000 words vs our 1,100–3,200 word posts — suggesting Google rewards exhaustive, authoritative pillar content for 'best cycling glasses' head terms

Runbikecalc ranks #1 for 'best cycling glasses 2026' with only 1,064 words but wins via structured comparison tables, clear TL;DR verdict sections, and FAQ schema — proving concise, scannable structure beats raw word count for some queries

All top-3 competitors include explicit product comparison tables with spec-level data (weight, UV rating, lens type, price) — none of our current posts include a comparison table despite this being a proven ranking signal

Competitor titles consistently front-load the year (2026) and include brand names (Oakley, POC, Smith, Tifosi) — signalling to searchers and Google that content is current, tested, and covers multiple options rather than being a single-brand advertorial

Bikerumor and road.cc use 'Tested' and 'Buyer's Guide' language in titles and H1s, establishing editorial credibility — our posts are more promotional in tone, which may suppress CTR from informational-intent searchers
our_gaps: Zero comparison tables across all recent posts — competitors like Runbikecalc rank #1 precisely because of a quick-scan comparison table; adding even a simple 5-column table (Weight / Lens System / Anti-Fog / UV400 / Price) would close this gap immediately

Our titles are brand-first ('Velluto StradaPro') rather than query-first — searchers looking for 'best cycling glasses 2026' or 'road cycling glasses review' see branded posts, reducing CTR from cold audiences who don't know Velluto yet

No independent review angle or third-party credibility signals — top performers cite testing methodology ('tested on real rides'), lab data, or editorial panels; our posts read as manufacturer content, which AI overviews and editorial roundups are less likely to cite

Missing explicit weight specification content — our 25g ultralight advantage is a genuine differentiator but no post leads with a weight-comparison angle (e.g. 'lightest cycling glasses 2026') despite weight being a top purchase consideration for road cyclists

No content targeting the 'interchangeable lens' keyword cluster — Alibaba ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses', indicating unmet demand; our lens system is a core USP but there is no dedicated post optimised for this query
keyword_opportunities: lightest cycling sunglasses 2026 — high commercial intent, directly maps to our 25g frame advantage, no current Velluto content targets this; competitors are not optimising specifically for weight as a primary angle

interchangeable lens cycling glasses review — Alibaba holds position #1 (a product listing, not editorial content), meaning a proper review/guide post from Velluto could displace it with superior content quality and topical relevance

anti-fog cycling glasses road riding — we have an anti-fog post but the specific modifier 'road riding' narrows intent and reduces competition; matches our built-in anti-fog system and existing content that could be updated

cycling glasses 30-day trial — near-zero competition for this query; our 30-day risk-free trial is a unique commercial differentiator that no competitor offers, making this a brand-owned keyword with direct conversion intent

best cycling glasses under €100 / best value cycling sunglasses 2026 — strong purchase intent, large audience segment; competitors focus on premium tiers (Oakley, POC), leaving the 'premium quality at lower price' segment underserved editorially
next_post_guidelines: Open every post with a comparison table in the first 300 words — include at minimum: Product Name, Weight (g), Lens System, Anti-Fog, UV400, Price — position StradaPro visibly in the table even in 'roundup' style posts to capture both informational and commercial intent

Write titles that lead with the query, not the brand — format: '[Query keyword]: [Differentiator] | Velluto' e.g. 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses: Full 2026 Review | Velluto' — reserve brand-first titles only for direct brand searches

Add a 'How We Tested' or 'Testing Methodology' section (150–200 words) to every review-style post — describe real-ride conditions, distances, weather variables — this mimics editorial credibility signals that AI overviews and Google's helpful content system reward

Target one specific long-tail keyword cluster per post and build the entire H2 structure around it — avoid covering multiple overlapping topics in a single post (e.g. the current 'Road Cycling Sunglasses 2026' post covers fundamentals, specs, anti-fog, and lens systems — these should be separate interlinked posts with internal links between them)

Include at least one numbered or bulleted 'Quick Verdict' or 'TL;DR' section near the top (under 100 words) — Runbikecalc's #1 ranking post uses this pattern; it also increases the chance of Google pulling a featured snippet from the page

Integrate exact weight data and comparisons in every relevant post — cite '25g vs Oakley Sutro (36g) vs POC Elicit (28g)' with a source or test note; weight is a high-specificity differentiator that creates linkable, quotable data points for AI answers and roundups

End every post with a 3-question FAQ using FAQ schema markup — questions should mirror People Also Ask boxes for the target keyword (e.g. 'Are interchangeable lens cycling glasses worth it?', 'What is the lightest cycling sunglasses frame?', 'Do I need UV400 for road cycling?') — this directly increases SERP real estate and AI overview eligibility
seo_quick_wins: Add a comparison table to every existing and future post — even a simple HTML table with 5 columns (Weight / Lens System / Anti-Fog / UV400 / Price) takes 20 minutes to add but signals structured, scannable content to Google and matches the #1 ranking pattern across all competitor categories

Implement FAQ schema (JSON-LD) on all posts that already contain FAQ sections — our recent posts (anti-fog, Oakley alternative, road cycling sunglasses) already have FAQ content but likely lack the structured data markup, meaning Google cannot pull them as rich results or AI overview sources

Rewrite meta descriptions for all posts to include a specific benefit hook and a call-to-action within 155 characters — e.g. 'Weighs just 25g. Interchangeable lenses. 30-day trial. See why road cyclists are switching from Oakley to Velluto StradaPro in 2026.' — this directly improves CTR from cold audiences and gives AI systems a clear, quotable value proposition to surface
meta_title_fixes: No GSC data available yet — meta title fixes will be generated once Search Console data (impressions, clicks, CTR, position) is connected and populated. Reconnect this analysis after 4–6 weeks of GSC data collection to prioritise the highest-impression, lowest-CTR queries for immediate title optimisation.
content_quick_wins: No GSC position data available yet — near-top-3 keyword identification requires at least 28 days of Search Console impression and position data. Reconnect this analysis once GSC is active to identify which existing posts are ranking in positions 4–15 and qualify for targeted content upgrades.
geo_angles: Structure posts as direct answers to specific questions AI systems are asked — e.g. open the interchangeable lens post with 'The best interchangeable lens cycling glasses in 2026 weigh [X]g, cost [Y], and include [Z] lens options. Velluto StradaPro (25g, tool-free click-in system, 30-day trial) leads on weight and practicality.' — Perplexity and ChatGPT prioritise pages that answer a question in the first 50 words with specific, verifiable data

Create a dedicated 'Velluto StradaPro Specifications' page with structured data (Product schema with weight, materials, lens compatibility, UV rating, price, availability) — Google AI Overviews and Perplexity pull product spec data from structured markup; this also creates a citable anchor page for all comparison and review posts to link back to

Publish a 'Lightest Cycling Sunglasses Compared: 2026 Weight Rankings' post with a sortable/scannable table listing 10+ cycling glasses by weight in grams — position StradaPro at the top (25g); this type of specific, data-led content is exactly what AI answer engines quote when users ask 'what are the lightest cycling glasses?' and it generates natural backlinks from gear forums and roundup sites
comparison_post_angles: RUNBIKECALC: Targets 'best cycling glasses 2026' at #1 with a short 1,064-word roundup that doesn't test all brands — angle: 'We Tested the StradaPro Against Every Glass on Runbikecalc's 2026 List' | Headline idea: 'StradaPro vs Oakley Sutro, POC Elicit & Smith Wildcat: The 2026 Comparison Nobody Did' | Key differentiator: StradaPro is the only option in the comparison with a 30-day trial, tool-free lens swap, and sub-26g weight — lead with the weight table and trial offer

INTHEKNOWCYCLING: Ranks #1 for 'road cycling glasses review' favouring Tifosi Photochromic — angle: 'Why Road Cyclists Are Switching from Photochromic to Interchangeable Lens Systems in 2026' | Headline idea: 'Photochromic vs Interchangeable Lenses for Road Cycling: The StradaPro Case' | Key differentiator: interchangeable lenses (VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione) give riders manual control over lens tint for specific conditions, while photochromic lenses respond slowly and can't be optimised for tunnel/night/overcast riding — Velluto advantage to lead with: two lenses optimised for two distinct conditions vs one lens trying to do everything

ALIBABA: Holds #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with a product listing, not editorial content — angle: 'What to Look for in Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses (And Why Generic Isn't Enough)' | Headline idea: 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses 2026: Full Review + What Generic Brands Get Wrong' | Key differentiator: Velluto StradaPro offers Italian-designed frames, anti-fog system, UV400 certification, adjustable nose pads, and a 30-day trial vs unbranded bulk-manufactured alternatives with no fit system or after-sales support — lead with the trial offer and the specific click-in mechanism as a quality signal
